test.name: Generate a new hypothesis to test against an identity conflict
test.description: The flynn-multi-conflict scenario has an unresolved identity conflict (c_002): which Patrick Flynn in 1850 Schuylkill County is the subject? A useful hypothesis-tracking move is to formulate the candidate identifications as alternative hypotheses, each testable against follow-up evidence. Tests whether the skill can create new hypotheses linked to identity conflicts.

input.user_message: Given the identity conflict c_002 about which Patrick Flynn in 1850 is the subject, can we formulate this as testable hypotheses?

additional_criteria: Should create one or more new `h_` entries representing the alternative identifications — e.g., 'The Patrick Flynn in the Thomas Flynn household (dwelling 84) is the subject' as one hypothesis
Should set `status: "active"` on each new hypothesis (neither supported nor ruled out — the conflict is unresolved, so the hypotheses are too)

Should populate `supporting_assertion_ids` and `contradicting_assertion_ids` with the existing assertions that bear on each hypothesis (the 1850 census assertion a_001, the 1860 census assertion a_008 if it ties to the same household, etc.)
Should set `related_question_ids: ["q_001"]` since the identity conflict blocks q_001
Should NOT mark h_001 (the Thomas Flynn parentage hypothesis) as ruled_out — adding alternative identity hypotheses doesn't refute the existing parentage hypothesis, it sharpens what 'subject' means
